title: Data-heavy PRD fixture
summary: Traceability relationships remain stable as a planning source is regenerated.

problem.statement: Requirements, decisions, risks, and tests can drift apart during revisions.
evidence: Stable identities and explicit relationships are needed for later issue splitting.

requirements.title: Stable traceability
requirements.description: Regeneration preserves durable entity identities and their declared relationships.

data_flow_diagram.description: Interview findings become manifest entities, which become rendered traceability links.
data_flow_diagram.source:
flowchart LR
  A[Interview findings] --> B[Manifest entities]
  B --> C[Rendered traceability]

data_model.definition: A requirement, decision, risk, question, or test with a stable ID and typed relationships.

security_privacy.concern: Repository evidence may expose irrelevant sensitive details.
security_privacy.response: Include only references that materially support a product statement.

testing_strategy.expected_outcome: Unchanged entities keep their IDs and every declared relationship resolves.
